description:
## Smell Shotgun Surgery (Fowler, *Refactoring*). One change to the rendered sidebar or one new sidebar assertion currently requires edits in every spec at once. During `bsm-kia.5` the sidebar basename `

` class changed from `text-muted` to `truncate`, which forced a synchronized selector swap in four specs plus the introduction of a new `expectSidebarWorkspaceAbsent` helper copy-pasted into each. ## Background The sidebar lives in `src/components/WorkspaceSelector.tsx`. Today the basename is selected with `"[aria-label='Workspace'] p.truncate"` (recently swapped from `p.text-muted`) and the four specs each: - Import `FIXTURE_SHARED_*` constants from the fixture module. - Inline the sidebar XPath/CSS selector into their own `expectSidebarWorkspacePath` and `expectSidebarWorkspaceAbsent` helpers. - Repeat the import/selector edit in lockstep when the rendered markup changes. `git show bsm-kia.5` shows the same selector string edited in `issue-list.atomic-switch.spec.ts:159`, `issue-list.success.spec.ts:359`, `:391`, and `:423` in a single commit, plus four new `expectSidebarWorkspaceAbsent` helpers introduced across the suite. ## What to build A single sidebar assertion module owns every selector and assertion the specs need to make about the rendered Workspace selector. Specs import named assertions; they never see the selector strings. - `e2e/issue-list/helpers/sidebar.ts` (or shared with the RPC helpers from the duplication-cleanup task) exports: - `expectCurrentWorkspace(basename | path)` — assert sidebar shows the expected workspace. - `expectNoCurrentWorkspace()` — assert the chooser is empty. - `expectRememberedWorkspace(basename)` — assert a remembered workspace appears in the chooser list. - Any selector the helper depends on lives inside the helper. - All four specs (`empty`, `issues`, `atomic-switch`, `restoration`) use only the named helpers; no spec contains a `[aria-label='Workspace']` selector literal. ## Acceptance criteria - [ ] `rg "\[aria-label='Workspace'\]|p\.truncate|p\.text-muted" e2e/issue-list/*.spec.ts` returns zero hits. - [ ] Every sidebar-related assertion in the suite is a single named helper call from `helpers/sidebar.ts` (or shared module). - [ ] Renaming the sidebar `

` class in `WorkspaceSelector.tsx` now touches one helper file, not four spec files. - [ ] `pnpm e2e:issue-list:complete` and `pnpm e2e:issue-list:isolation` still pass. ## Coordination This task overlaps with the duplicated-RPC cleanup (the sidebar helpers naturally live next to the typed-RPC helpers). Land them together if practical, or land this one first if the duplication cleanup wants to keep its scope limited to the typed-RPC plumbing. ## Out of scope - Redesigning the Workspace selector UX. - Replacing WebdriverIO selectors with a different selector library. - Adding new assertions beyond what exists today.
title: Stop scattering sidebar selector changes across the e2e specs
